front: Novel: the butler Stevens takes a motoring trip to visit Miss Kenton, lent a car by his new American employer Mr. Farraday
back: The Remains of the Day (Kazuo Ishiguro)

front: Novel: Stevens formerly served the Nazi sympathizer Lord Darlington at Darlington Hall

front: Novel: the protagonist resolves to practice 'bantering' and hopes to surprise his employer
back: The Remains of the Day (Kazuo Ishiguro)

front: Novel: a woman says 'no turning back the clock now,' making the protagonist realize 'at that moment, my heart was breaking'
back: The Remains of the Day (Kazuo Ishiguro)

front: Novel: the protagonist illustrates 'dignity' with the story of a man discovering a tiger under a dining table
back: The Remains of the Day (Kazuo Ishiguro)

front: Novel: the protagonist is caught reading a sentimental romance novel and claims he is trying to 'develop his control of the English language'
back: The Remains of the Day (Kazuo Ishiguro)

front: Novel: a fictional radio show called Twice a Week or More and the Hayes Society for butlers appear
back: The Remains of the Day (Kazuo Ishiguro)

front: Novel: Kathy, Tommy, and Ruth are clones raised to be organ donors at Hailsham boarding school; the title comes from a fictional Judy Bridgewater song
Novel: clones search for 'possibles' — their genetic originals — and one asserts 'we're modeled from trash'
Novel: clones hope for 'deferrals' by proving they are in love, but Madame reveals these never existed
Novel: Madame collects student art in a Gallery to prove clones have souls
Novel: Madame tearfully watches the narrator pretend to cradle a baby while listening to a Judy Bridgewater song
Novel: the solar-powered Artificial Friend Klara destroys a polluting 'Cootings Machine' to cure Josie's illness
Novel: Josie and her neighbor Rick play the 'bubble game,' filling in blank speech bubbles in each other's drawings
Novel: amnesia-inducing mist in post-Arthurian Britain caused by the dragon Querig; Wistan kills Querig and Sir Gawain
Novel: the elderly couple Axl and Beatrice search for their son; ends with a boatman refusing to ferry both to an island
Novel: the concert pianist Ryder arrives in an unnamed European city to perform but somehow never manages to; features the drunken conductor Brodsky who uses an ironing board as a crutch
Novel: the painter Masuji Ono reflects on post-WWII life; drinks at Mrs. Kawakami's bar; the title refers to ukiyo-e
back: An Artist of the Floating World (Kazuo Ishiguro)
work: An Artist of the Floating World

front: Novel: Ono turns his painting 'Complacency' into a propaganda work titled 'Eyes on the Horizon'
back: An Artist of the Floating World (Kazuo Ishiguro)
work: An Artist of the Floating World

front: Novel: detective Christopher Banks believes his parents were kidnapped in Shanghai for fighting against opium suppliers; 'Yellow Snake' is revealed to be his uncle Philip
Author: writes lyrics for jazz singer Stacey Kent, including The Summer We Crossed Europe in the Rain
